title: How Iran secretly acquired facial recognition technology through a Russian company
subhed: An investigation coordinated by Forbidden Stories, in partnership with Le Monde, reveals that an Iranian company purchased and then resold the Russian surveillance software FindFace to various entities within the regime.

title: France enters Iran conflict in 'strictly defensive' posture
subhed: In a solemn address on Tuesday evening, Emmanuel Macron announced, among other measures, the deployment of the aircraft carrier Charles-de-Gaulle to the eastern Mediterranean, confirming the likelihood of a wider regional conflict in the Middle East.

title: Beirut hotel hit, six killed as Israel launches 'broad wave of strikes'
subhed: Lebanon's health ministry said that strikes on two towns south of Beirut had killed six people and wounded eight, while Israel stated on Wednesday that it has launched a 'broad wave of strikes' on targets in Iran.

title: 'Dubai, a city of spectacle and a global hub, is now caught up in geopolitics'
subhed: Geographer Roman Stadnicki, who specializes in Gulf countries' urban landscapes, discusses the impact of Iranian strikes on the United Arab Emirates' principality.

title: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhed: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.
byline: By Chloé Hoorman and Marie Jégo

title: France's Iranian artistic diaspora torn between anxiety and joy
subhed: Since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, artists in the diaspora have found themselves dreaming of better days, even as they fear a rising death toll.

title: Israel unleashes unlimited firepower in Iran and Lebanon
